data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0015/sessions/0010-horizon-T14

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.

1	/-

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveMorphism
8	
9	/-!
10	# Serre finiteness for the section graded module (statement)
11	
12	The deep remaining input of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
13	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, inbox `I-0109`): for a projective scheme over
14	a field carrying a very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent sheaf `F`, the
15	section graded ring `R(X, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian, the section
16	graded module `M(X, F, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module over it,
17	and each graded component is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector space.
18	
19	This file gives the **honest statement** against the projectivity vocabulary
20	of `Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean` (the `IsProjectiveWith` closed-immersion
21	predicate — very ampleness in the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118`), as a
22	named typed `sorry` leaf: the proof is Serre's theorem ([Hartshorne] II.5.17
23	ff., [Nitsure] §1) and is genuinely deep (multi-session; it needs the
24	pushforward-to-projective-space reduction and cohomological finiteness on
25	`ℙⁿ`).
26	
27	Compared with the blueprint node, which hypothesises an *ample* `L` and
28	passes to a Veronese power, the Lean statement hypothesises the very ample
29	form directly (`IsProjectiveWith`) — this is the form in which the
30	Quot-scheme endgame ([Nitsure] §5) consumes it.
31	
32	Blueprint: `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`
33	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
34	-/
35	
36	open CategoryTheory Limits
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	/-- The structural `κ`-algebra structure on the global sections of a scheme
43	over `Spec κ` (the absolute analogue of `Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`). -/
44	def Scheme.Hom.structuralSectionsHom {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [CommRing κ]
45	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) : κ →+* Γ(X, ⊤) :=
46	  ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of κ)).inv ≫ π.appTop).hom
47	
48	/-- **Serre finiteness for the section graded module**
49	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`; [Nitsure] §1, [Hartshorne] II.5.17 ff.):
50	for `π : X ⟶ Spec κ` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L`
51	and a coherent (finitely presented) sheaf `F` on `X`,
52	
53	1. the section graded ring `⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian;
54	2. the section graded module `⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module
55	   over it;
56	3. each component `Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector
57	   space (via the structural `κ`-action).
58	
59	NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): the geometric heart of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
60	(inbox `I-0109`), awaiting the pushforward-to-`ℙⁿ` reduction and
61	cohomological finiteness of coherent sheaves on projective space. -/
62	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [Field κ]
63	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) (L F : X.Modules)
64	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
65	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
66	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L)))
67	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
68	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule F L
69	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L))
70	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L)))
71	    ∧ ∀ m : ℕ,
72	        letI := Module.compHom (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m)
73	          π.structuralSectionsHom
74	        FiniteDimensional κ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m) := by
75	  sorry
76	
77	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SerreTwist
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotFunctorDef
9	
10	/-!
11	# Projective morphisms carrying a very ample line bundle
12	
13	Mathlib v4.31 has no projective-morphism class and no (very) ampleness
14	vocabulary.  Following the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment
15	`C-0002`), this file defines:
16	
17	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf n₀ S m` — the Serre twist `O(m)` on the
18	  relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`, the pullback of
19	  `ProjTwist.serreTwist` from the integral model along `toProjInt`;
20	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange` — `O(m)` commutes with base
21	  change of the ambient projective space;
22	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith π L` — the **projective-with-`L`** predicate:
23	  `π : X ⟶ S` factors through a closed immersion `i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)`
24	  over `S` with `L ≅ i^* O(1)`;
25	
26	with the stability facts the Quot-scheme endgame consumes:
27	
28	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.isProper` — projective morphisms are proper;
29	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.comp_isClosedImmersion` — a closed immersion
30	  into a projective scheme is projective (carrying the restricted bundle);
31	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` — stability under base change
32	  (carrying the pulled-back bundle).
33	
34	Everything is at `Scheme.{0}`: the Serre twist rests on the descent engine
35	`Scheme.Modules.glue`, which is universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
36	(`GlueDescent.lean:934`).
37	
38	Blueprint: `def:twisting_sheaf`, `def:projective_with`,
39	`lem:projective_with_proper`, `lem:projective_with_closed_immersion`,
40	`lem:projective_with_base_change`
41	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
45	
46	noncomputable section
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace ProjectiveSpace
51	
52	variable (n₀ : Type) (S : Scheme.{0})
53	
54	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`:
55	the pullback of the glued twisting sheaf on the integral model. -/
56	def twistingSheaf (m : ℕ) : (ℙ(n₀; S)).Modules :=
57	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (toProjInt n₀ S)).obj (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
58	
59	variable {S} {S' : Scheme.{0}}
60	
61	/-- The Serre twist commutes with base change of the ambient projective
62	space: `(map g)^* O(m) ≅ O(m)`. -/
63	def twistingSheafBaseChange (g : S' ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) :
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (map n₀ g)).obj (twistingSheaf n₀ S m) ≅
65	      twistingSheaf n₀ S' m :=
66	  Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (map_toProjInt n₀ g) (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
67	
68	end ProjectiveSpace
69	
70	/-- `π : X ⟶ S` is a **projective morphism carrying the line bundle `L`**
71	([Nitsure] §5, [EGA II] 5.5): there are a closed immersion
72	`i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)` over `S` and an isomorphism of `L` with the
73	pullback of the Serre twist `O(1)`.  This is the faithful encoding of
74	"projective with relatively very ample `L`" settled in inbox `I-0118`. -/
75	def Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith {X S : Scheme.{0}} (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules) :
76	    Prop :=
77	  ∃ (d : ℕ) (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)),
78	    IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π ∧
79	      Nonempty (L ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback i).obj
80	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1))
81	
82	namespace Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
83	
84	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}} {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
85	
86	/-- **Projective morphisms are proper**: a closed immersion is proper, the
87	structural morphism of projective space is proper, and properness is stable
88	under composition. -/
89	theorem isProper (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsProper π := by
90	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, -⟩ := h
91	  haveI := hi
92	  rw [← hcomp]
93	  infer_instance
94	
95	/-- **Composition with a closed immersion**: if `π` is projective carrying
96	`L` and `j` is a closed immersion into `X`, then `j ≫ π` is projective
97	carrying `j^* L`. -/
98	theorem comp_isClosedImmersion (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {Y : Scheme.{0}}
99	    (j : Y ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion j] :
100	    (j ≫ π).IsProjectiveWith ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj L) := by
101	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
102	  haveI := hi
103	  refine ⟨d, j ≫ i, inferInstance, by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp], ?_⟩
104	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫
105	    Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (rfl : j ≫ i = j ≫ i)
106	      (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)⟩
107	
108	/-- The comparison morphism from the base-changed total space into the
109	base-changed projective space. -/
110	private def baseChangeLift {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
111	    (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)) (hcomp : i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π) :
112	    pullback π g ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') :=
113	  (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).lift
114	    (pullback.fst π g ≫ i) (pullback.snd π g)
115	    (by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp, pullback.condition])
116	
117	/-- **Stability under base change**: if `π : X ⟶ S` is projective carrying
118	`L` and `g : S' ⟶ S`, then the base change `X ×_S S' ⟶ S'` is projective
119	carrying the pullback of `L`. -/
120	theorem baseChange (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) :
121	    (pullback.snd π g).IsProjectiveWith
122	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).obj L) := by
123	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
124	  haveI := hi
125	  refine ⟨d, baseChangeLift g i hcomp, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
126	  · -- the comparison square exhibits the lift as the base change of `i`
127	    have h1 : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S')
128	        = pullback.snd π g := IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
129	    have hsq : IsPullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp) (pullback.fst π g)
130	        (ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g) i := by
131	      have hbig : IsPullback
132	          (baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S'))
133	          (pullback.fst π g) g (i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S)) := by
134	        rw [h1, hcomp]
135	        exact (IsPullback.of_hasPullback π g).flip
136	      exact IsPullback.of_right hbig
137	        (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _)
138	        (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).flip
139	    exact MorphismProperty.of_isPullback hsq.flip hi
140	  · exact IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
141	  · refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).mapIso e ≪≫
142	      Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _ :
143	        baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
144	          = pullback.fst π g ≫ i).symm
145	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) ≪≫ ?_⟩
146	    -- collapse `(lift ≫ map)^* O(1)` to `lift^* (map^* O(1))`, then use the
147	    -- base-change isomorphism of the twist
148	    exact (Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso
149	        (rfl : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
150	          = _) (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)).symm ≪≫
151	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp)).mapIso
152	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange (Fin (d + 1)) g 1)
153	
154	end Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
155	
156	end AlgebraicGeometry

322	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\) by \(L_s\) on \(X_s\) (\cref{def:sheafModuleTwist}), and
323	  the dimension is taken with respect to the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector-space
324	  structure on sections over the fibre (\cref{def:fiber_sections_module}).
325	  This is exactly the graded Hilbert function of the section graded module
326	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); it uses
327	  only global sections \(H^0\).
328	\end{definition}
329	
330	\begin{lemma}
331	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
332	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
333	  \leanok
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
335	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing, def:projective_with}
336	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
337	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
338	  % Polynomials" (read from
339	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
340	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
341	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
342	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
343	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
344	  %  the function
345	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
346	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
347	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
348	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
349	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
350	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
351	  Let \(X_s\) be projective over the field \(\kappa(s)\) carrying the very
352	  ample line bundle \(L_s\) (\cref{def:projective_with}: a closed immersion
353	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\) with
354	  \(L_s \cong \mathcal{O}(1)|_{X_s}\)), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a
355	  coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
356	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
357	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
358	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
359	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
360	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
361	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
362	  (For an ample \(L_s\) the same conclusions follow by passing to a very
363	  ample Veronese power; the very ample form stated here is the one consumed
364	  by the Quot-scheme construction.)
365	\end{lemma}
366	
367	\begin{proof}
368	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
369	  The closed immersion \(i : X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\)
370	  with \(L_s \cong i^* \mathcal{O}(1)\) reduces the claims to projective
371	  space: pushing forward along \(i\) identifies
372	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) with
373	  \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^N, (i_* \mathcal{F}_s)(m))\), and \(i_* \mathcal{F}_s\)
374	  is coherent. By Serre's theorem the section
375	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
376	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a quotient of the polynomial ring in \(N+1\)
377	  variables in large degrees), so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
378	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), Serre's theorem yields that
379	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
380	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
381	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
382	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
383	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
384	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
385	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
386	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
387	\end{proof}
388	
389	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
390	  graded series]
391	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
392	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
393	  \mathlibok
394	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
395	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
396	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
397	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
398	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
399	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
400	  equals \(h(n)\):
401	  \[
402	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
403	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
404	  \]
405	  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
406	  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
407	  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
408	  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
409	  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
410	\end{lemma}
411	
412	\begin{lemma}[Additivity of \(\dim_\kappa\) on a short exact sequence
413	  (rank--nullity)]
414	  \label{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
415	  \lean{Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank}
416	  \mathlibok
417	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
418	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity}).}
419	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
420	  \(W \subseteq V\) a subspace. Then
421	  \[
422	    \dim_\kappa (V / W) + \dim_\kappa W \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
423	  \]
424	  Equivalently, \(\dim_\kappa = \operatorname{finrank}_\kappa\) is additive on a
425	  short exact sequence \(0 \to W \to V \to V/W \to 0\) of finite-dimensional
426	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. This is the additivity that converts the degreewise
427	  short exact sequences of graded components into additive relations among the
428	  coefficients of the Hilbert series.
429	\end{lemma}

435	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Relative projective space `ℙ(n; S)`
10	
11	Mathlib v4.31 has the absolute `Proj` of a ℕ-graded ring together with the
12	properness package for `Proj.toSpecZero` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean`),
13	but no relative projective space over an arbitrary base scheme.  Following the
14	`AffineSpace` pattern (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean`) and the
15	encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment `C-0002`), this file defines
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpace n S` (notation `ℙ(n; S)`): the relative
18	  projective space with homogeneous coordinates indexed by `n : Type u`, i.e.
19	  the pullback of the integral model
20	  `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n] = Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ))`
21	  along `S ⟶ ⊤_ Scheme`;
22	* the structural morphism `ℙ(n; S) ↘ S` (a `CanonicallyOver` instance) with
23	  `IsProper` (hence `IsSeparated`, `UniversallyClosed`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
24	  `QuasiCompact`) derived by base change from the Mathlib instances on
25	  `Proj.toSpecZero`;
26	* functoriality `ProjectiveSpace.map` in the base and the base-change square
27	  `ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map`.
28	
29	The key observations making the transfer work: the degree-zero part of the
30	homogeneous coordinate ring is the copy of `ULift ℤ` given by the constants
31	(`MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule_zero`), so `Spec (𝒜 0)` is terminal and
32	`terminal.from (Proj 𝒜)` factors as `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` followed by an
33	isomorphism; and for finitely many variables the coordinate ring is of finite
34	type over its degree-zero part, which is exactly the hypothesis of the Mathlib
35	properness instances.
36	
37	Blueprint: `def:projective_space`, `lem:projective_space_proper`
38	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
39	-/
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
42	
43	noncomputable section
44	
45	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
46	
47	universe u
48	
49	/-- The total-degree grading on the homogeneous coordinate ring
50	`ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]` of the integral model of projective space (with `ULift ℤ`
51	coefficients for universe polymorphism, as in `AffineSpace`). -/
52	local notation3 "𝒫[" n "]" => homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ)
53	
54	/-- The total-degree grading is a graded ring structure.  Mathlib deliberately
55	does not register `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` as a global instance (other
56	weightings of the same polynomial ring exist); for the fixed homogeneous
57	coordinate ring of projective space we register it project-wide. -/
58	instance (n : Type u) : GradedRing 𝒫[n] :=
59	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
60	
61	variable (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u})
62	
63	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is the projective space over `S` with homogeneous coordinates
64	indexed by `n : Type u` (e.g. `ULift (Fin (m + 1))` for `ℙ^m_S`).  It is the
65	base change to `S` of the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`. -/
66	def ProjectiveSpace (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u}) : Scheme.{u} :=
67	  pullback (terminal.from S)
68	    (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
69	
70	namespace ProjectiveSpace
71	
72	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is the projective `n`-space over `S`. -/
73	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation "ℙ(" n "; " S ")" => ProjectiveSpace n S
74	
75	instance canonicallyOver : (ℙ(n; S)).CanonicallyOver S where
76	  hom := pullback.fst _ _
77	
78	lemma over_eq_fst : ℙ(n; S) ↘ S = pullback.fst _ _ := rfl
79	
80	/-- The projection from `ℙ(n; S)` to the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`. -/
81	def toProjInt : ℙ(n; S) ⟶ Proj 𝒫[n] := pullback.snd _ _
82	
83	lemma toProjInt_eq_snd : toProjInt n S = pullback.snd _ _ := rfl
84	
85	@[reassoc]
86	lemma over_terminal_comp :
87	    (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) ≫ terminal.from S = toProjInt n S ≫ terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]) :=
88	  pullback.condition
89	
90	section GradeZero
91	
92	/-- The degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring is the copy of
93	`ULift ℤ` given by the constants. -/
94	lemma bijective_algebraMap_gradeZero :
95	    Function.Bijective
96	      (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)) := by
97	  constructor
98	  · intro a b hab
99	    have := congrArg Subtype.val hab
100	    simpa using MvPolynomial.C_injective n (ULift.{u} ℤ) this
101	  · intro x
102	    obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := Submodule.mem_one.mp
103	      ((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule_zero (σ := n) (R := ULift.{u} ℤ)).le x.2)
104	    exact ⟨y, Subtype.ext (by simpa using hy)⟩
105	
106	/-- The degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring, as a ring, is
107	`ULift ℤ`. -/
108	def gradeZeroRingEquiv :
109	    ULift.{u} ℤ ≃+* homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0 :=
110	  RingEquiv.ofBijective
111	    (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0))
112	    (bijective_algebraMap_gradeZero n)
113	
114	/-- `Spec` of the degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring is a
115	terminal scheme. -/
116	def specGradeZeroIsTerminal :
117	    IsTerminal (Spec (.of (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0))) :=
118	  specULiftZIsTerminal.{u}.ofIso
119	    (Scheme.Spec.mapIso (gradeZeroRingEquiv n).toCommRingCatIso.op).symm
120	
121	instance :
122	    IsIso (terminal.from (Spec (.of (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)))) :=
123	  isIso_of_isTerminal (specGradeZeroIsTerminal n) terminalIsTerminal _
124	
125	/-- For finitely many variables, the homogeneous coordinate ring is of finite
126	type over its degree-zero part — the hypothesis of the Mathlib properness
127	package for `Proj.toSpecZero`. -/
128	instance [Finite n] :
129	    Algebra.FiniteType (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
130	      (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) := by
131	  haveI := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R := ULift.{u} ℤ)
132	    (S := homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
133	    (A := MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) fun r => rfl
134	  exact Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType
135	    (R := ULift.{u} ℤ) (S := homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
136	    (A := MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
137	
138	end GradeZero
139	
140	section Instances
141	
142	instance isProper_terminalFrom_proj [Finite n] :
143	    IsProper (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) := by
144	  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Proj.toSpecZero (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))]
145	  infer_instance
146	
147	instance isSeparated_terminalFrom_proj :
148	    IsSeparated (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) := by
149	  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Proj.toSpecZero (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))]
150	  infer_instance
151	
152	/-- The structural morphism of relative projective space is proper.  Its
153	parent classes (`IsSeparated`, `UniversallyClosed`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
154	hence also `QuasiCompact`) follow by instance projection. -/
155	instance isProper_over [Finite n] : IsProper (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) :=
156	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ (isProper_terminalFrom_proj n)
157	
158	instance isSeparated_over : IsSeparated (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) :=
159	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ (isSeparated_terminalFrom_proj n)
160	
161	end Instances
162	
163	section Functorial
164	
165	variable {S} {T : Scheme.{u}}
166	
167	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is functorial in the base `S`. -/
168	def map (f : S ⟶ T) : ℙ(n; S) ⟶ ℙ(n; T) :=
169	  pullback.map _ _ _ _ f (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp)
170	
171	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
172	lemma map_fst (f : S ⟶ T) :
173	    map n f ≫
174	        pullback.fst (terminal.from T)
175	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) =
176	      pullback.fst (terminal.from S)
177	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) ≫ f :=
178	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
179	
180	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
181	lemma map_snd (f : S ⟶ T) :
182	    map n f ≫
183	        pullback.snd (terminal.from T)
184	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) =
185	      pullback.snd (terminal.from S)
186	        (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) :=
187	  (pullback.lift_snd _ _ _).trans (Category.comp_id _)
188	
189	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
190	lemma map_over (f : S ⟶ T) : map n f ≫ ℙ(n; T) ↘ T = (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) ≫ f := by
191	  rw [over_eq_fst, over_eq_fst]
192	  exact pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
193	
194	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
195	lemma map_toProjInt (f : S ⟶ T) : map n f ≫ toProjInt n T = toProjInt n S := by
196	  rw [toProjInt_eq_snd, toProjInt_eq_snd]
197	  exact (pullback.lift_snd _ _ _).trans (Category.comp_id _)
198	
199	@[simp]
200	lemma map_id : map n (𝟙 S) = 𝟙 ℙ(n; S) := by
201	  apply pullback.hom_ext
202	  · exact (map_fst n (𝟙 S)).trans
203	      ((Category.comp_id _).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm)
204	  · exact (map_snd n (𝟙 S)).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm
205	
206	@[reassoc, simp]
207	lemma map_comp {U : Scheme.{u}} (f : S ⟶ T) (g : T ⟶ U) :
208	    map n (f ≫ g) = map n f ≫ map n g := by
209	  apply pullback.hom_ext
210	  · exact (map_fst n (f ≫ g)).trans <| ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans <|
211	      (congrArg (· ≫ g) (map_fst n f).symm).trans <| (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
212	        congrArg (map n f ≫ ·) (map_fst n g).symm).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
213	  · exact (map_snd n (f ≫ g)).trans <| ((map_snd n f).symm.trans <|
214	      congrArg (map n f ≫ ·) (map_snd n g).symm).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
215	
216	/-- Relative projective space commutes with base change: the square
217	
218	```
219	ℙ(n; S) ⟶ ℙ(n; T)
220	   |          |
221	   S    ⟶    T
222	```
223	
224	is a pullback. -/
225	lemma isPullback_map (f : S ⟶ T) :
226	    IsPullback (map n f) (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) (ℙ(n; T) ↘ T) f := by
227	  have hbig : IsPullback (toProjInt n S) (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S)
228	      (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n])) (terminal.from S) :=
229	    (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]))).flip
230	  have hright : IsPullback (toProjInt n T) (ℙ(n; T) ↘ T)
231	      (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n])) (terminal.from T) :=
232	    (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (terminal.from T) (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]))).flip
233	  refine IsPullback.of_right ?_ (map_over n f) hright
234	  rw [map_toProjInt, terminal.comp_from f]
235	  exact hbig
236	
237	end Functorial
238	
239	end ProjectiveSpace
240	
241	end AlgebraicGeometry
